title: Chicken Coop Run for Shed Coop
summary: A framed 2x6/2x4 chicken run with chicken-wire walls and a hinged access door, sized to attach to a shed-style coop.
description:
This run is built as a true framed structure rather than a wire fence on posts — full 2x6 wall framing with 2x4 cross supports every few feet, which is what lets the chicken wire simply staple to something solid on all four sides instead of needing its own separate post-and-wire fence system. That framing also carries the door, which is why the door gets built with pocket holes as its own small, square unit rather than just a wire-covered opening cut into a wall panel.

Cutting the door pieces from a single 10 ft 2x4 rather than an 8 ft board is a real lumber-conservation call worth keeping — three 35" door pieces come out of one 10-footer with very little waste, where an 8 ft board would leave you buying a second one for a single extra piece.

tools.note: Attaches the chicken wire to the frame.

materials.name: Pine, 2x4, 10 ft
materials.note: Cut three 35" door pieces from this one board to conserve lumber.

steps.title: Build the two walls
steps.body: Build the two long wall frames from the wall framing and cross supports, spacing the cross supports evenly for chicken-wire attachment points.

steps.title: Attach the tops and ends
steps.body: Attach the long wall runners across the tops and ends of the two wall frames to tie the whole run together into one structure.

steps.title: Frame the door opening
steps.body: Build the door framing into the structure where the access door will hang.

steps.title: Cover with chicken wire
steps.body: Run chicken wire over the top and sides of the structure, stapling it to the framing and cross supports.

steps.title: Build and hang the door
steps.body: Build the door from the door stiles and rails, joined with pocket holes. Attach chicken wire to the front of the door, then hang it in the framed opening with the gate hinges.

images.alt: Framed chicken run with chicken-wire walls attached to a shed coop
